data.typographic_voice: Warm, humanist, precise. Confident headers that feel like a competent friend talking; neutral, legible body for menus and macros; unambiguous, aligned figures for calories, macros and rupees.
data.sentence_case_rule: Headlines and UI copy are sentence case, never Title Case or ALL CAPS shouting. Rasa is calm and warm; the type reflects it.

display.name: General Sans
display.role: Headlines, hero copy, section headers, plan titles.
display.rationale: General Sans is modern and confident but softly humanist — it carries the 'competent friend who cooks' voice without corporate coldness or diet-app severity. Satoshi is the sanctioned fallback with the same tone.

body.name: Inter
body.role: Body copy, menu items, UI labels, email text.
body.rationale: Inter is engineered for small sizes on mobile — tall x-height, open counters — so restaurant names, dish descriptions and helper text stay legible at 14-16px. It steps back and lets the food and the plan lead.

data.name: Inter (tabular figures)
data.role: Calories, macros (P/C/F grams), prices, the spend ledger, budget bars.
data.rationale: Numbers that must align in columns and update in place — macro readouts, per-slot prices, the running monthly total — use tabular figures so digits never jitter as values change. IBM Plex Mono is the fallback where true tabular Inter is unavailable.

display_lg.use: Marketing section headers, in-app plan hero.

overline.use: Eyebrows and section markers — the one sanctioned uppercase use.

data_lg.use: Hero numbers — monthly cap, total protein, spend so far.

number_and_currency_handling.tabular_figures: All numeric readouts use 'tnum' so columns align and in-place updates don't shift width.
number_and_currency_handling.currency: Rupee shown as ₹ prefix with no space (₹9,000); Indian digit grouping (lakh/thousand comma style, e.g. ₹1,20,000) in ledgers and totals.
number_and_currency_handling.macros: Grams shown as compact chips — 'P 42g · C 40g · F 18g' — always paired with a 'kcal' value; all figures carry the estimate treatment (see disclaimer).
number_and_currency_handling.estimate_disclaimer: Nutrition figures render at caption size in Kettle Slate with a preceding '~' or 'est.' — e.g. 'est. 520 kcal'. FSSAI-safe: wellness estimates, never precise or medical claims.
number_and_currency_handling.rounding: Calories rounded to nearest 10; macros to nearest whole gram; prices to whole rupees.

mobile.notes: App-first; most Rasa reading happens on phones.

hierarchy: General Sans for headlines and plan/section titles; Inter for everything read at length.
Keep sentence case throughout — no Title Case, no shouting caps except the overline eyebrow.
Limit to three visible heading levels per screen for calm.
emphasis: Use weight (500/600) and Basil Green for emphasis, not italics or underlines.
Ripe Tomato is a colour of action (confirm), not of running text — never colour body copy tomato.
numbers: Every macro or price uses the tabular data style so it aligns and updates cleanly.
Every nutrition figure carries the '~/est.' estimate treatment.
Indian comma grouping for rupee amounts.
accessibility: Minimum body size 16px; minimum interactive size 14px in tabular data chips.
Minimum contrast 4.5:1 for body; large display can use 3:1.
